passage: The town's annual harvest festival attracts families from across the county every October. Vendors sell homemade pies, fresh apple cider, and seasonal crafts along the main street. This year, the festival committee [1]_____ a pumpkin-carving contest to the list of activities.

passageMetadata.source: Adapted from a community events listing

stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
explanation: This question tests verb tense selection. 'This year' combined with the past-tense context (the committee already made the decision) requires simple past. The singular subject 'the festival committee' takes 'added.'

distractorRationale.A: Correct. Simple past matching the completed action.
distractorRationale.B: Present singular—wrong tense for a completed decision.
distractorRationale.C: Base form/plural—wrong tense and disagrees with singular subject.
distractorRationale.D: Participle—cannot serve as main verb without auxiliary.
